showyouridentity.flag.rainbow.name: Rainbow Pride Flag
showyouridentity.flag.rainbow.descriptio: The modern version features horizontal bars of red, orange, yellow, green, blue, and violet. Each color represents a different aspect of life and community, such as healing, sunlight, and nature.
showyouridentity.flag.progress_pride.des: This flag builds on the traditional rainbow flag by adding a left-facing chevron to explicitly emphasize inclusion and ongoing progress. The chevron features black and brown stripes to represent marginalized LGBTQ+ communities of color. These stripes are accompanied by the light blue, pink, and white colors of the Transgender Pride Flag.
showyouridentity.flag.lesbian.name: Sunset Lesbian Pride Flag
showyouridentity.flag.lesbian.descriptio:
A lesbian is generally defined as a woman or non-binary person who experiences romantic, emotional, and/or sexual attraction to women or feminine-aligned individuals.
The community adopted this flag to be more inclusive of gender non-conforming and transgender lesbians. The orange stripes represent themes such as nonconformity, independence, and community. The pink stripes represent serenity, love, and femininity. All of these themes are centered around a white stripe, which represents unique relationships to womanhood.
showyouridentity.flag.gay.name: Gay Men Pride Flag (MLM Flag)
showyouridentity.flag.gay.description:
A gay person is generally defined as a man or masculine-aligned person who experiences romantic, emotional, and/or sexual attraction to men or masculine-aligned individuals.
This flag was designed as a counterpart to the lesbian flag. The top green and teal stripes symbolize community, healing, and joy. The bottom blue and purple stripes symbolize pure love, strength, and diversity. A white stripe at the center represents transgender, non-binary, and gender nonconforming gay men.
showyouridentity.flag.bisexual.name: Bisexual Pride Flag
showyouridentity.flag.bisexual.descripti:
A bisexual person is someone who experiences romantic, emotional, and/or sexual attraction to more than one gender.
This flag was created to provide the bisexual community with its own distinct symbol of visibility, separate from the general rainbow flag. The top pink stripe represents attraction to one's own gender. The bottom blue stripe represents attraction to different genders. The overlapping purple stripe in the middle represents attraction across the gender spectrum.
showyouridentity.flag.transgender.name: Transgender Pride Flag
showyouridentity.flag.transgender.descri:
A transgender person is someone whose gender identity differs from the sex they were assigned at birth.
The flag's symmetrical design ensures that it is always displayed correctly, symbolizing finding correctness in one's own life. The light blue stripes represent the traditional color for baby boys, the pink stripes represent the traditional color for baby girls, and the white center stripe represents those who are intersex, transitioning, or who consider their gender to be neutral or undefined.
showyouridentity.flag.intersex.name: Intersex Pride Flag
showyouridentity.flag.intersex.descripti:
An intersex person is born with sex characteristics that do not fit typical binary notions of male or female bodies.
This flag intentionally avoids gendered colors like blue and pink. The yellow background and purple circle represent wholeness and completeness. They also symbolize the right to be exactly how God or nature made you, representing bodily autonomy and self-determination.
showyouridentity.flag.asexual.name: Asexual Pride Flag
showyouridentity.flag.asexual.descriptio:
An asexual person experiences little to no sexual attraction to others, though they may still experience romantic, emotional, or aesthetic attraction.
A community vote was held to choose this flag to represent the various facets of the ace spectrum. The black stripe represents asexuality; the gray stripe, demisexuality and gray-asexuality; the white stripe, non-asexual allies and partners; and the purple stripe, community.
showyouridentity.flag.asexual.credit: Designed by Asexual Visibility and Education Network (AVEN) user @standup in 2010.
showyouridentity.flag.aromantic.name: Aromantic Pride Flag
showyouridentity.flag.aromantic.descript:
An aromantic person experiences little to no romantic attraction to others. This means they generally do not experience "crushes" or desire romantic partnerships. However, they often deeply value platonic relationships.
This flag is the most widely recognized version. The dark and light green stripes represent the aromantic spectrum (since green is the opposite of red, which is traditionally associated with romance). The white stripe represents platonic love and friendship. The gray and black stripes represent the sexuality spectrum.
showyouridentity.flag.aroace.name: Aroace Pride Flag
showyouridentity.flag.aroace.description:
An aroace person identifies as both aromantic and asexual, meaning they experience little to no romantic attraction and little to no sexual attraction.
Rather than simply combining the green and purple flags, this flag incorporates aspects of both identities into a unique, unified design. The orange stripes represent the community and unique nature of aroace love and relationships. The white stripe represents wholeness and peace. The blue stripes represent the aroace identity and spectrum.
showyouridentity.flag.aroace.credit: Designed by Tumblr user @bi-aroace (now known as Jacqueline) in 2018.
showyouridentity.flag.agender.name: Agender Pride Flag
showyouridentity.flag.agender.descriptio:
An agender person is someone who identifies as having no gender, a neutral gender, or an absence of gender identity entirely.
This flag represents genderlessness and neutrality. The black and white stripes represent an absence of gender, the gray stripes represent semi-genderlessness, and the central green stripe represents non-binary identities because green is the inverse of purple, which is a mix of the traditional gender colors, pink and blue.
showyouridentity.flag.agender.credit: Designed by Salem X (under the alias @transvestia) in 2014.
showyouridentity.flag.non_binary.name: Non Binary Pride Flag
showyouridentity.flag.non_binary.descrip:
A non-binary person is someone whose gender identity does not fit strictly within the binary categories of male or female, which can include identifying as both, neither, or fluctuating between genders.
The flag was designed to complement, not replace, the genderqueer flag. The yellow stripe signifies individuals whose gender falls outside the binary, the white stripe denotes those with multiple or all genders, the purple stripe designates those whose gender is a blend of male and female, and the black stripe stands for those who identify as gender neutral.
showyouridentity.flag.demiboy.name: Demiboy Pride Flag
showyouridentity.flag.demiboy.descriptio:
A demiboy is someone who identifies partially, but not fully, as a man, boy, or masculine person, regardless of their sex assigned at birth.
The flag's muted colors symbolize a partial identity. The gray stripes symbolize the gray areas of gender identity; the blue stripes represent masculinity or manhood; and the central white stripe represents non-binary or agender identities.
showyouridentity.flag.demigirl.name: Demigirl Pride Flag
showyouridentity.flag.demigirl.descripti:
A demigirl is someone who identifies partially, but not fully, as a woman, girl, or feminine person, regardless of their sex assigned at birth.
The flag's muted colors symbolize a partial identity. The gray stripes symbolize the gray areas of gender identity; the pink stripes represent femininity or womanhood; and the central white stripe represents non-binary or agender identities.
showyouridentity.flag.genderfluid.name: Genderfluid Pride Flag
showyouridentity.flag.genderfluid.descri:
A genderfluid person is someone whose gender identity is not fixed and fluctuates over time, shifting between different genders such as male, female, non-binary, or agender.
This flag encompasses the dynamic and changing nature of gender fluidity. The pink stripe represents femininity; the white stripe, all genders; the purple stripe, a combination of masculinity and femininity; the black stripe, a lack of gender; and the blue stripe, masculinity.
showyouridentity.flag.genderqueer.name: Genderqueer Pride Flag
showyouridentity.flag.genderqueer.descri:
A genderqueer person is someone whose gender identity falls outside of, extends beyond, or rejects the traditional male-female binary. This term can be used as a specific identity or as an umbrella term, similar to "non-binary."
This flag features lavender, white, and dark green stripes. The lavender stripe represents a mixture of blue and pink, symbolizing androgyny and queer identities. The white stripe represents agender or gender-neutral identities. The dark green stripe represents identities that stand completely outside the binary.
showyouridentity.flag.demisexual.name: Demisexual Pride Flag
showyouridentity.flag.demisexual.descrip:
A demisexual person experiences sexual attraction to another person only after forming a strong emotional bond with them. It sits on the asexual spectrum because attraction is not felt inherently or instantly.
This flag uses the same colors as the asexual flag, but in a unique arrangement. The black chevron represents asexuality; the gray stripe, the demisexual spectrum; the white stripe, sexuality and allies; and the purple stripe, community.
showyouridentity.flag.demisexual.credit: Designed by Asexual Visibility and Education Network (AVEN) user Sonofofallon in 2010.
showyouridentity.flag.demiromantic.name: Demiromantic Pride Flag
showyouridentity.flag.demiromantic.descr:
A demiromantic person experiences romantic attraction to another person only after forming a strong emotional bond with them. It sits on the aromantic spectrum meaning romantic feelings do not develop based on initial appearance or surface-level interactions.
This flag adapts the layout of the demisexual flag but swaps the colors to reflect the aromantic identity. The black chevron represents the aromantic spectrum; the gray stripe, the demiromantic spectrum; the white stripe, platonic love and friendship; and the green stripe, aromanticism.
showyouridentity.flag.demiromantic.credi: Designed by an anonymous Tumblr user in 2015.
showyouridentity.flag.pansexual.name: Pansexual Pride Flag
showyouridentity.flag.pansexual.descript:
A pansexual person is someone who is romantically, emotionally, and/or sexually attracted to people regardless of their gender identity. This is often described as being gender-blind or as being attracted to people of all genders.
This flag was designed to increase visibility for the pansexual community, separate from the bisexual flag. The pink stripe represents attraction to people who identify as female, the blue stripe represents attraction to people who identify as male, and the yellow stripe represents attraction to non-binary, genderqueer, agender, or genderfluid individuals.
showyouridentity.flag.pansexual.credit: Designed by Jasper V. (under the username @evandragonfly) in 2010.
showyouridentity.flag.omnisexual.name: Omnisexual Pride Flag
showyouridentity.flag.omnisexual.descrip:
An omnisexual person is someone who experiences romantic, emotional, and/or sexual attraction to people of all genders. Unlike pansexuals, who are often described as "gender-blind," omnisexuals recognize and are aware of a person's gender. The gender may still play a role in their attraction.
This flag uses shades of pink and blue to represent the gender spectrum. The light and dark blue stripes represent attraction to men and masculine genders; the light and dark pink stripes represent attraction to women and feminine genders; and the central dark purple stripe represents attraction to nonbinary and gender expansive identities.
showyouridentity.flag.omnisexual.credit: Designed by the user @bastard-of-the-skies (aka Pastellight) in 2015.
showyouridentity.flag.polysexual.name: Polysexual Pride Flag
showyouridentity.flag.polysexual.descrip:
A polysexual person is attracted to multiple genders, but not necessarily all of them. Unlike pansexuality, which is attraction regardless of gender, or omnisexuality, which is attraction to all genders, polysexuality is attraction to many genders while excluding others.
This flag was designed to represent the specific nature of multi-gender attraction. The pink stripe represents attraction to people who identify as female, the blue stripe represents attraction to people who identify as male, and the green stripe represents attraction to people who identify outside the traditional gender binary, such as non-binary, genderfluid, or agender individuals.
showyouridentity.flag.polyamory.name: Polyamory Pride Flag
showyouridentity.flag.polyamory.descript:
A polyamorous person is someone who is open to or engages in having multiple romantic and/or emotional relationships simultaneously with the informed consent of all partners involved.
The modern flag was designed to replace the 1995 tricolor flag with a more cohesive symbol. The light blue stripe represents openness and honesty; the magenta stripe, love and passion; and the dark purple stripe, the polyamorous community. The white chevron on the left points forward to symbolize progression, and the central gold heart represents the core value of love and emotional connection.
showyouridentity.flag.bear_brotherhood.d:
In the gay, bisexual, and queer male communities, a "bear" is a subculture that typically describes a hairy, physically large, burly, or heavy-set man.
This flag represents inclusivity across the global community. Its horizontal stripes feature a gradient of colors (dark brown, orange-rust, golden yellow, tan, white, gray, and black) representing the fur and skin tones of bears and humans worldwide. A black bear paw print sits in the upper-left canton, symbolizing the distinct identity and strength of the brotherhood.
showyouridentity.flag.femboy.name: Femboy Pride Flag
showyouridentity.flag.femboy.description:
A femboy is someone who identifies as an man or masculine but expresses their gender in traditionally feminine ways through clothing, behavior, or aesthetics. This gender expression challenges conventional male roles.
This flag features a symmetrical color gradient designed to highlight the intersection of male identity and feminine expression. The dark pink stripes on the outside and the lighter pink stripes on the inside symbolize femininity and feminine presentation. The white stripes represent non-binary individuals who also embrace the femboy aesthetic. The central light blue stripe represents the underlying male or masculine-aligned identity.
showyouridentity.flag.transfem.name: Transfeminine Pride Flag
showyouridentity.flag.transfem.descripti:
A transfeminine person is someone who was assigned male at birth but identifies more closely with femininity, womanhood, or a feminine gender identity. This term encompasses transgender women, as well as non-binary, genderfluid, and demigirl individuals who express or identify as feminine.
The flag expands on the colors of the traditional transgender flag. The light blue outer stripes represent male assignment at birth, transitioning inward through progressively deeper shades of pink to symbolize the journey toward and embrace of a proud feminine identity.
showyouridentity.flag.transmasc.name: Transmasculine Pride Flag
showyouridentity.flag.transmasc.descript:
A transmasculine person is someone who was assigned female at birth but identifies more closely with masculinity, manhood, or a masculine gender identity. This term encompasses transgender men, as well as non-binary, genderfluid, and demiboy individuals who express or identify as masculine.
The flag expands on the colors of the traditional transgender flag. The light pink outer stripes represent female assignment at birth, transitioning inward through progressively deeper shades of blue to symbolize the journey toward and embrace of a proud masculine identity.
showyouridentity.flag.neptunic.name: Neptunic Pride Flag
showyouridentity.flag.neptunic.descripti:
A neptunic person is someone who experiences attraction to women, feminine-aligned non-binary people, and neutral or unaligned non-binary people. This orientation explicitly excludes attraction to men, masculine-aligned non-binary individuals, and masculinity. This term is often used by non-binary individuals to describe their attraction without using binary terms such as "lesbian" or "straight".
This flag uses an oceanic color palette inspired by the planet Neptune. The top teal and aqua stripes represent feminine and woman-aligned identities. The middle pastel blue and light aqua stripes symbolize neutral and unaligned non-binary individuals. The bottom lavender and purple stripes represent the unique, queer nature of this attraction.
showyouridentity.flag.neptunic.credit: Designed by an anonymous Tumblr user in 2017.
showyouridentity.flag.uranic.name: Uranic Pride Flag
showyouridentity.flag.uranic.description:
A uranic person is someone who experiences attraction to men, masculine-aligned non-binary people, and neutral or unaligned non-binary people. This orientation explicitly excludes attraction to women, feminine-aligned non-binary individuals, and femininity. This term is often used by non-binary individuals to describe their attraction without using binary terms such as "gay" or "straight".
This flag features a sky-themed color scheme inspired by Uranus, the Greek god of the sky. The top four stripes are a gradient of medium to soft pastel blues representing men and masculine-aligned identities. The bottom cream and soft tan-orange stripes represent individuals who are neutral, unaligned, or non-binary and are included within this attraction.
showyouridentity.flag.uranic.credit: Designed by an anonymous Tumblr user in 2017.
showyouridentity.flag.sapphic.name: Sapphic Pride Flag
showyouridentity.flag.sapphic.descriptio:
A sapphic person is a woman or non-binary individual attracted to women and feminine-aligned people. This inclusive umbrella term encompasses lesbians, bisexual women, pansexual women, and queer women, focusing on the shared experience of female-to-female attraction, regardless of whether they are exclusively attracted to women.
The flag is a modern adaptation of older historical symbols. The top and bottom pink stripes represent love, romance, and womanhood. The middle white stripe represents safety, peace, and inclusion of gender-expansive individuals within the community. In the center is a violet flower, a historic symbol of sapphic love dating back to Sappho's poetry in ancient Greece. She wrote of her lovers wearing garlands of violets.
showyouridentity.flag.sapphic.credit: Designed by Tumblr users @pride-flags and @lesbian-herstory in 2015.
showyouridentity.flag.sapiosexual.name: Sapiosexual Pride Flag
showyouridentity.flag.sapiosexual.descri:
A sapiosexual person experiences primary romantic or sexual attraction toward intelligence and the human mind. For these individuals, an intellectual connection, stimulating conversation, or a person's mental depth is the foundation of attraction, taking priority over physical appearance or gender identity.
This flag represents the various aspects of an intellectual connection. The top green stripe represents growth, development, and the expansion of the mind. The middle brown stripe represents stability, depth, and the grounded nature of deep thought. The bottom blue stripe represents knowledge, logic, and intellectual exploration.
showyouridentity.flag.straight_ally.name: Straight Ally Flag
showyouridentity.flag.straight_ally.desc:
Allies leverage their privilege to combat homophobia, biphobia, transphobia, and systemic inequalities, creating safe and inclusive spaces for LGBTQ+ individuals.
This flag combines symbols of heterosexual identity and queer pride. The background consists of alternating black and white horizontal stripes, which historically represent the straight pride flag. In the center is a large, stylized capital "A" filled with the traditional six-color LGBTQ+ rainbow. The "A" stands for "ally" and signifies the community's active efforts to provide support and protection to the queer community.
showyouridentity.flag.straight_ally.cred: Designed by an unknown person in the late 2000s.
showyouridentity.flag.trans_ally.name: Trans Ally Flag
showyouridentity.flag.trans_ally.descrip:
Allies leverage their privilege to combat homophobia, biphobia, transphobia, and systemic inequalities, creating safe and inclusive spaces for LGBTQ+ individuals.
Mirroring the design concept of the straight ally flag, this flag combines heterosexual/cisgender identity with transgender pride. The background consists of alternating black and white horizontal stripes, which historically represent the straight pride flag. In the center is a large, stylized capital "A" filled with light blue, pink, and white colors of Monica Helms’ traditional transgender pride flag. The "A" stands for "ally" and signifies the community's active efforts to provide support and protection to the trans community.
showyouridentity.flag.trans_ally.credit: Designed by an unknown person in the late 2010s.
showyouridentity.midnightconfig.comment_: The slur filter needs to be based on the structure of awdev1/better-profane-words on GitHub
showyouridentity.midnightconfig.title: Show Your Identity
showyouridentity.midnightconfig.renderTh: Show Nametag above Head in Third Person
showyouridentity.midnightconfig.commentS: Some serverside mods require workarounds, that make the pronouns and flags apply on serverside. These settings are only for these cases.
